title: ArtViva
text: Artviva School's all-in-one online solution, seamlessly integrating tuition payments, application submissions, and informative pages.

Powered by React and Node.js, this comprehensive web platform offers a holistic experience, enhanced by Google Analytics insights. Experience convenience, functionality, and a captivating user interface, all within Artviva School's integrated digital hub.
description: Artviva School's integrated web platform, built with React on the front-end and powered by Node.js on the backend, combines a range of functionalities to provide a seamless online experience. Students can conveniently make tuition payments using our secure payment system, Liqpay, seamlessly integrated into the backend deployed on Heroku. Additionally, prospective students can effortlessly submit their applications through the website, streamlining the enrollment process for both students and the school.

To ensure an optimal user experience and continuously enhance the website, Artviva School utilizes Google Analytics. This powerful tool enables detailed tracking and analysis of website traffic, user behavior, and performance metrics. By gaining insights into visitor demographics, popular pages, and user engagement, Artviva School can make data-driven decisions to improve the website's content, design, and functionality. Google Analytics empowers Artviva School to provide an up-to-date and user-centric website that meets the evolving needs of students, parents, and the wider community.

With a user-friendly interface designed with React and Bootstrap, Artviva School's platform ensures a smooth and responsive experience. In addition to these interactive features, the website also includes informative pages like 'About', 'Team', and 'Contacts', offering visitors a deeper understanding of Artviva School's mission, faculty, and contact information. Experience a holistic online solution that combines functionality, convenience, and an engaging user experience, all within Artviva School's integrated web platform.

title: Huma
text: Experience the power of Next.js in our performance-optimized website project. By leveraging the latest advancements in Webpack 5 and Next.js, we've achieved lightning-fast UI and build/load times.

With the migration to Next.js and the use of TypeScript, our website delivers exceptional performance and developer-friendly workflows. The integration of Netlify CMS streamlines content management, empowering editors with easy updates.
description: Our project began with the goal of enhancing performance in both UI and build/load times. Initially built using Handlebars and Webpack 4, we later upgraded to the more efficient Webpack 5 to achieve significant performance improvements. However, to further optimize the website and enhance development experience, we rebuilt the entire project using Next.js and Typescript.

By leveraging Next.js, a powerful React framework, we achieved remarkable performance enhancements, thanks to its server-side rendering capabilities and built-in optimizations. This allowed for faster page load times and improved user experience. The integration of Typescript ensured type safety, enhanced code quality, and streamlined development workflows.

To simplify content management and ensure easy updates, we implemented Netlify CMS, a headless CMS solution. This allowed editors to seamlessly manage site content, reducing reliance on developers for routine content updates. The result is a website that is easily maintainable by developers and user-friendly for editors, fostering collaboration and efficient content management.

With Next.js, Typescript, and Netlify CMS at the core, our project delivers exceptional performance, developer-friendliness, and a streamlined content management experience. Embrace a high-performing website that strikes the perfect balance between speed, maintainability, and ease of use.

title: Sharlushka
text: Sharlushka is a Yahtzee-inspired dice game built as a Progressive Web App. Roll, drag, and score your way through a classic combo-chasing session – solo or against a real opponent in multiplayer. Play as a guest or create an account to track your stats across games and watch your progress on the stats page. Installable on any device, works offline, and built with React, Redux Toolkit, and WebSockets under the hood.
description: Sharlushka is a full-stack Yahtzee-inspired dice game built as a Progressive Web App. It started as a personal project to explore real-world frontend architecture – and grew into a fairly complete game platform. Gameplay follows the classic Yahtzee scoring model: roll five dice, hold the ones you like, re-roll up to twice, then pick a scoring combo. The tricky part is the score calculation logic – detecting combinations like a full house, straights, or Yahtzee across a live dice board required careful implementation and is tested with unit tests.

Multiplayer is built on WebSockets (Socket.IO). Two players can join a shared game session, take turns, and see live game state updates. The frontend manages connection lifecycle, turn syncing, and opponent state through a dedicated multiplayer slice and custom hooks. Auth and stats – players can register to persist their results. Each completed game is saved to the backend and displayed as charts on the stats page using Recharts – breakdowns by combo type, win rates, and score trends over time. PWA – the app is installable on any device and uses Workbox to cache assets and handle service worker updates gracefully, including prompting the user when a new version is available.

Tech used: React 19, TypeScript, Redux Toolkit + RTK Query, React Router 7, dnd-kit for drag-and-drop dice interaction, Framer Motion for animations, react-hook-form + Zod for form validation, Recharts, Webpack 5 with a custom config, Sass modules, and Netlify for hosting. The main challenge was the scoring logic – and later, wiring up a stateful real-time multiplayer experience on top of an app originally built for solo play.

title: Ongage
text: Ongage is a full-featured email marketing platform built with React 19 and TypeScript. It lets marketers manage subscriber lists, design and send campaigns, build automated workflows, and validate email lists — all from a single, modern web app. The platform integrates AI throughout: a natural-language analytics interface for querying campaign performance, an AI-powered email template generator, and a context-aware AI assistant that can navigate the app and act on behalf of the user via chat.
description: Ongage is a large-scale, multi-tenant email marketing SaaS platform. I built and maintained the React front end that gives marketers full control over every stage of an email program — from subscriber acquisition to post-campaign analysis.

The platform covers the full email marketing lifecycle. Marketers can import, segment, and export subscriber lists, managing contact statuses and field-level data across millions of contacts. From there they can create, schedule, and A/B test email and SMS campaigns with real-time send statistics, or build sophisticated automated sequences — welcome series, re-engagement flows, and more — using a node-based drag-and-drop workflow editor. A unified vendor management interface lets teams configure and switch between multiple ESP and SMTP delivery providers. Supporting tools include bulk email validation against a third-party service with status tracking and downloadable results, plus analytics dashboards covering campaign overview charts, aggregate performance tables, and insight breakdowns across key deliverability metrics.

AI is woven throughout the product in three distinct layers. The AI Analytics feature lets users query their campaign data in plain English — typing a question like 'which campaigns had the highest open rate last month?' returns structured results rendered as interactive charts alongside a conversational answer, with a thumbs-up/down feedback loop to improve accuracy over time. For content creation, an AI Email Template Generator guides users through a short brief — choosing tone, target audience, primary goal, language, and email type — and produces a fully styled HTML template that can be previewed and saved directly as a new message. Finally, a CopilotKit-powered AI chat sidebar acts as a persistent in-app assistant: it knows which page the user is on, can navigate to any section of the app, and can create, update, or delete platform entities such as campaigns, segments, automations, and vendor connections on the user's behalf, automatically refreshing the relevant UI data caches after each action via MCP (Model Context Protocol).

On the engineering side, the codebase follows a strict modular feature architecture where each domain owns its API clients, Zustand stores, TanStack Query hooks, and UI components, keeping pages thin and business logic co-located. A three-variant TanStack Table system handles server-paginated, client-paginated, and context-free tables with persistent column visibility and ordering. Firebase authentication with token-refresh interceptors is wired into every API and AI call. Strict TypeScript, ESLint with exhaustive-deps enforced as an error, Prettier, and Husky pre-commit hooks kept the codebase consistently clean across a large team.

title: Rosterly
text: Agency Roster is a full-stack platform that connects marketing brands with creative and media agencies. Marketers can submit a search brief, receive an AI-generated shortlist of matched agencies ranked by compatibility, schedule introductory calls with selected candidates, and track the entire process through a unified dashboard. Agencies on the other side manage their profile, respond to incoming invitations, and submit proposals — all within the same application.
description: The frontend is a single-page application built with React 19 and TypeScript, bundled with Vite and styled using a combination of Tailwind CSS utility classes and CSS Modules written in SCSS syntax. Routing is handled by React Router v7, and all server state is managed through TanStack Query v5 paired with Axios, which provides consistent caching, background refetching, and optimistic update patterns across the entire data layer. Data tables throughout the admin and marketer interfaces are powered by TanStack Table v8, offering column-level filtering, pagination, and sortable headers without sacrificing control over rendering.

Authentication is delegated to Stytch, whose React SDK handles session management, magic links, and password flows. The resulting session token is stored in a React context and consumed by a shared hook throughout the component tree, enabling granular role-based rendering for the three distinct user roles: admin, marketer, and agency. Form handling relies on Formik with Yup schemas for validation, including a shared configuration layer that enforces consistent field constraints across the application.

The AI-powered agency matching feature accepts a marketer's brief — optionally with attached documents sent as multipart form data — and returns a scored list of agencies, each accompanied by a capability map visualised as a radar chart using Recharts. The same charting library drives the analytics dashboards, which surface engagement metrics and historical search data. Completed AI search reports can be exported to PDF through an html2canvas and jsPDF pipeline that applies a separate stylesheet to the document during capture, isolating print-specific layout from the interactive view.

The chemistry meeting workflow lets marketers select agencies from an AI report, propose available time slots with timezone-aware date handling via date-fns and date-fns-tz, and send invitations in bulk. Agencies receive those invitations as RFPs, can accept or decline them, upload supporting files through a dedicated dropzone modal, and request alternative time slots if none of the proposed ones work. The entire notification layer uses a server-sent events stream, with unread counts and real-time updates surfaced through a slide-in drawer.

Accessibility is a first-class concern: interactive components such as modals, dropdowns, select fields, and date pickers are built on top of React Aria Components, which provides ARIA attribute management, keyboard navigation, and focus trapping out of the box. Loading states use react-loading-skeleton to maintain layout stability during data fetches, and toast notifications via react-hot-toast give users consistent feedback for mutations. Code quality is enforced through ESLint, Prettier, and Husky pre-commit hooks with lint-staged, ensuring the TypeScript, formatting, and linting checks all pass before any commit lands.
